About: It is about a Gulf Coast day.
It is about the transition from day to night.
It is about life and time on Horn Island.
It is about the beauty of a day on the Coast.
It is about the environment of the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Walter Anderson.
It is associated with Horn Island.
It is associated with the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
BuiltIn: It was built in an extension on the south side of the home after 1939.
ConsistsOf: It consists of painted wooden walls.
It consists of oil paints, house paint, and tempura.
It consists of representations of different times of day on each wall.
It consists of a zinnia on the ceiling.
CreatedAround: It was created around sometime after 1951.
CreatedBy: It was created by Walter Anderson.
CreatedFor: It was created for illustrating a Gulf Coast day.
It was created for portraying life and time on Horn Island.
It was created for a private expression of an artist.
CreatedIn: It was created in Anderson's cottage.
It was created in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.
CreatedTo: It was created to illustrate a Gulf Coast day.
It was created to show the transition from day to night.
It was created to mentally transport himself to Horn Island.
It was created to portray life and time on the island.
It was created to chronicle the transition from night to day.
It was created to be a hymn to the light and beauty of a day on the Coast.
HasA: It has bright colors.
It has vibrant images.
It has floor-to-ceiling murals.
It has a double window facing south.
It has single windows on the east and west walls.
It has a north wall dominated by the back of the fireplace and chimney.
It has a door that led into the main cottage.
It has a wooden chest containing Anderson’s transcription of Psalm 104.
It has a crowing rooster perched atop the east window frame.
It has a hillside with goats to the left of the east window.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the transition from day to night.
It is characterized by representations of different times of day on each wall.
It is characterized by symbolism inspired by Psalm 104.
It is characterized by the use of light as an organizing principle.
HeldIn: It was held in the Walter Anderson Museum of Art.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the environment in which it was created.
IsA: It is painted on the wooden walls of Anderson’s cottage.
It is referred to as “Little Room.”
It is referred to as “Creation at Sunrise.”
It is a hymn to the light and beauty of a day on the Coast.
It is a monumental zinnia on the ceiling.
It is an element of its setting, the Mississippi Gulf Coast.
It is an intensely private expression of an artist.
KnownAs: It is known as “Creation at Sunrise.”
KnownFor: It is known for illustrating a Gulf Coast day.
It is known for its vibrant images.
It is known for its symbolism inspired by Psalm 104.
LocatedIn: It is located in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.
It is located in the Walter Anderson Museum of Art.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.
PartOf: It is part of the Walter Anderson Museum of Art.
UsedFor: It is used for illustrating a Gulf Coast day.
It is used for showing the transition from day to night.
It is used for mentally transporting himself to Horn Island.
It is used for portraying life and time on the island.
It is used for chronicling the transition from night to day.
It is used for a hymn to the light and beauty of a day on the Coast.
UsedIn: It is used in the Walter Anderson Museum of Art.
UsedTo: It is used to illustrate a Gulf Coast day.
It is used to show the transition from day to night.
It is used to mentally transport himself to Horn Island.
It is used to portray life and time on the island.
It is used to chronicle the transition from night to day.
It is used to be a hymn to the light and beauty of a day on the Coast.
